content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 28, 1991

Martin Koppel
P.O. Box 4055
Lancaster, CA  93539

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-91-439

Dear Mr. Koppel:

     You have requested advice concerning your reporting
obligations under the conflict of interest disclosure provisions
of the Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTIONS

     1.  When is your leaving office statement due?

     2.  Are you subject to any prohibitions after leaving
office?

CONCLUSIONS

     1.  Your leaving office statement is due within 30 days of
leaving office.

     2.  The Political Reform Act's revolving door provisions on
post-government employment do not apply to local elected
officials.

FACTS

     You will not seek reelection to the board of directors for
the Quartz Hill Water District. The election will be held
November 5, 1991. The newly elected member will be sworn into
office either at the November 7, 1991 meeting or the December 5,
1991 meeting.


     1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Martin Kopel
page 2

ANALYSIS

Section 87302 requires officials who are designated in a
conflict of interest code to file a leaving office statement
within 30 days of leaving office.

Assuming that your term ends when the newly elected member is
sworn into office, your leaving office statement must be filed no
later than 30 days after the new member is sworn in. Enclosed is
a Form 730 for your use. However, you will need to contact the
filing officer for the Quartz Hill Water District to obtain a copy
of your disclosure category. Your disclosure category will
indicate the types of investments, interests in real property,
income, and business positions you must disclose.

Sections 87400-87405 ("revolving door provisions") apply
only to state officials. You may wish to contact your County
Counsel's office to determine if your county has adopted a local
ordinance on post-government employment which would affect you as
a former board member.

If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[signature: Colleen McGee]

by: Colleen McGee
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division

Enclosure

sections.analysis:
Section 87302 requires officials who are designated in a conflict of interest code to file a leaving office statement within 30 days of leaving office.

Assuming that your term ends when the newly elected member is sworn into office, your leaving office statement must be filed no later than 30 days after the new member is sworn in. Enclosed is a Form 730 for your use. However, you will need to contact the filing officer for the Quartz Hill Water District to obtain a copy of your disclosure category. Your disclosure category will indicate the types of investments, interests in real property, income, and business positions you must disclose.

Sections 87400-87405 ("revolving door provisions") apply only to state officials. You may wish to contact your County Counsel's office to determine if your county has adopted a local ordinance on post-government employment which would affect you as a former board member.
